frameworks.name: low-rank recurrent neural networks
frameworks.one_line: connectivity decomposed into low-rank structure shapes emergent low-dimensional population dynamics

frameworks.name: neural population dynamics on low-dimensional manifolds
frameworks.one_line: task computations as trajectories through low-dimensional neural subspaces

frameworks.name: random matrix theory of connectivity spectra
frameworks.one_line: eigenvalue analysis extended to mixed low-rank-plus-sparse-random connectivity

core_question: How does randomly sparsifying connections affect the low-dimensional dynamics of low-rank recurrent neural networks?
key_assumptions: low-rank connectivity generates structured low-dimensional dynamics
cortical connectivity is sparse rather than all-to-all
sparsified low-rank matrices behave like low-rank plus full-rank random component
manipulations: level of random sparsification of connections
rank of the underlying connectivity structure
key_findings: sparsification makes the connectivity matrix formally full-rank
eigenspectra split into a continuous bulk and isolated outliers
spectral form mirrors that of low-rank plus full-rank random matrices
low-dimensional dynamics are preserved even at high sparsity
interpretations: structured low-dimensional computation is robust to biologically realistic sparsity
low-rank RNN framework remains applicable to sparse cortical networks
